For the 28-day Abandoned Hope, just like the 6-week or the 42-day, you are going to meet with them at week 3. I highly suggest scheduling this as a mandatory thing on their nutrition orientation. Whatever you sit down with them initially, that way it's on the books, it's scheduled, there's no way getting out of it. Now, a couple things that I will suggest different from the 28-day is you need to absolutely over-deliver on the fulfillment side. And there's a previous video about this where it talks about, I would want to ask a question every week about what's the biggest change you've seen. How are you liking it? Getting them to tell you how their life is different every week on top of the metrics and numbers because you might have someone whose weight only goes down a pound or two, but all of a sudden for the first time in their life they've walked with their kids outside or they've done these little things that you want to build on and touch on in this Abandoned Hope meeting. Still following the sales funnel that's talked about in the sales training earlier, nothing changes there, but you want to really impress that just in three short weeks, look what they've accomplished besides the scale and obviously you don't want to quit and get them to be like, no, I don't want to quit because who wants to quit, right? Now, that being said, the pricing structure for the bribe or for the offer of if you sign up today is going to be the same, but I wanted to break this down so everyone can see it. I'm actually going to include a calculator below that you can put your pricing into and we will help figure this out for you. Now, so let's just say the 28-day challenge is $3.99. Now, obviously you have control of what the price. This is our suggested price. Then you would say something along the lines of on your price sheet, normal pricing would be desired weekly price times 52 plus 3.99. So that would be the total for the year. So example, if you wanted to get $49 a week from the client, you would take 49 times 52 plus 3.99. It would equal $2947 or $56 weekly. Or if you're going to do it monthly, which we suggest weekly, but if you're doing monthly, it'd be $199 times 12 plus 3.99 equals $2,787. Now, which would be $232 a week. So when you present them, you're like, hey, Susie, so here's the deal. We want to really keep you on. You've done so great. If you decide to continue on today, what we're going to do is apply the money you've put down towards membership. So instead of paying $56 a week, you pay $49. Or instead of paying $232 a month, you're going to pay $199. What I would suggest in the 28 days, it is a little bit quicker. There's a little bit more of a rush to get them committed to something. And so I would absolutely add a stack onto this and say, hey, you know what, right now you're doing the 28 day meal plan, we're going to put you into the XYZ program. We're going to give you the XYZ offer. You're going to get a shirt, a blank, a blank. Try to add a value stack into this that gives them more incentive to come on. So swag is great. If you have an extra program that's starting or getting ready to internal play, add them into that, but just one more thing so they don't feel too rushed into this. Because in a six week program, they typically have a little more time. But if you've done the work right in the past three weeks of filling them, taking care of them, checking in on them, making sure they're seeing results, this should be no problem. And it's no different than traditional abandoned hope.
